last_report:
### CONFIRMED — Miami Heat ML
- Match: Miami Heat vs Philadelphia 76ers | Tip: 2026-03-30T23:00 UTC
- Pick: Miami Heat ML | Scout odds: 2.08 | Commit odds: 2.06 | Move: -0.02
- ML: H:2.06 / A:1.78 | ATS: -2.0 pts (1.91/1.91) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €55.50 | Pot: €114.33 | Conf: 62/100 | EV: 0.2772
- Decision: Minor 0.02 line move against (within 0.10 threshold, no investigation required). Both anchor players (Embiid, Butler) confirmed active on NBA official injury report — no new absences for either team since Scout. PHI NetRtg -0.2 with hot streak (L10 7-3, W% .554 > .550) remains the primary edge signal. Hot streak fade rule explicitly triggers: +10 confidence applied. Miami home advantage and positive NetRtg +2.2 supports the pick. EV 0.277 is strong. Confirmed at Medium tier (62 confidence), 15% bankroll stake.

---

### CANCELLED — Boston Celtics ML (Draft Pick 002)
- Reason: Franchise player Jayson Tatum confirmed OUT per NBA official injury report. Scout's own reasoning already identified rejection — confidence falls to 40 (65 base - 25 franchise player penalty), below minimum ML confidence floor of 50. Negative EV at -0.132. Additionally, Nikola Vucevic (new injury, not in Scout data), Ron Harper Jr., and Neemias Queta are all confirmed OUT at commit time, further deepening Boston's depletion. No path to confidence ≥ 50 on Boston ML.
- Odds at cancel: 2.17 (was 2.10 at Scout time — odds improved but irrelevant given rejection criteria)

---

### LATE PICK CONFIRMED — Atlanta Hawks -2.5
- Match: Atlanta Hawks vs Boston Celtics | Tip: 2026-03-30T23:30 UTC
- Pick: Atlanta Hawks -2.5 | Scout odds: N/A (late pick) | Commit odds: 1.88 | Move: N/A
- ML: H:1.71 / A:2.17 | ATS: -2.5 pts (1.88/1.94) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €55.50 | Pot: €104.34 | Conf: 60/100 | EV: 0.128
- Decision: New late-breaking edge. Boston confirmed WITHOUT Tatum (OUT), Vucevic (OUT), Harper Jr. (OUT), Queta (OUT) — four players unavailable, including franchise player. Atlanta at full strength (only minor G-League and illness absences). Tatum's OUT is confirmed per NBA official (not Questionable/GTD), satisfying the injury-dependent pick rule — pick confirmed only because OUT is official. Atlanta home court advantage. Spread 1.88 within target range (1.80-2.30). All gates satisfied: confidence 60 ≥ 60 minimum, EV 0.128 > 0.05, time gate 63 min > 20 min, quantity gate (1st and only late pick). Atlanta ML at 1.71 showed negative EV (-0.008), but the spread at 1.88 generates sufficient EV given Atlanta covering just 2.5 points at home vs depleted road opponent.

---

### SLATE SUMMARY
Two picks committed totaling €111.00 (30.0% of €370.01 bankroll) — well within the 70% exposure cap. The core thesis centers on PHI's negative NetRtg regression risk in Miami (confirmed, both anchor players active) and Atlanta exploiting Boston's historic injury crisis at home on the spread. Draft pick 002 (BOS ML) was correctly self-cancelled by Scout and formally cancelled here; a superior alternative on the same game (ATL -2.5) was identified through the late scout process with stronger EV (0.128) and a confirmed injury catalyst.

At commit time, odds improved to 2.38 (+0.13 in our favour), consistent with news that Luka Doncic (Questionable, Right Hip) and LeBron James (Questionable, Left Foot Arthritis) are both uncertain for Lakers â€” two franchise-level players potentially unavailable. Austin Reaves also Questionable. Heat are missing Jaquez Jr. (confirmed OUT, was Questionable at Scout), Andrew Wiggins (OUT, new), and Terry Rozier (OUT, new), but their core rotation (Butler, Adebayo) remains intact. Net injury impact heavily favours Heat: Lakers potentially missing two franchise players outweighs Heat's role-player absences. Books have moved from 2.25 to 2.38, partially pricing in LAL risks, but 2.38 still offers strong value. EV at commit odds: (0.53 Ã— 2.38) - 1 = 0.2614 = 26.1%, well above 0.05 threshold. Odds within ML valid range (1.65â€“2.50). Confidence maintained at 53 (Speculative): LAL injury upside offset by Heat's additional absences. Stake: 10% of â‚¬1000 = â‚¬100. Original stake unchanged per rules (do not increase even on improved odds). Total exposure: 10% of bankroll.

Detroit (53-20, L10: 8-2, NetRtg +7.8) is the materially superior team by record and efficiency vs Minnesota (45-28, L10: 5-5, NetRtg +3.6). Anthony Edwards (Minnesota franchise player) is confirmed OUT with patellofemoral pain, joined by Jaden McDaniels and Ayo Dosunmu. Cade Cunningham is also OUT for Detroit (pneumothorax), applying -10 confidence and -20% stake reduction per franchise_player_rules. Despite both teams missing key players, Detroit's underlying quality advantage (8-game record gap, +4.2 NetRtg differential, far superior L10 form) combined with Minnesota's triple-injury blow justifies confidence in Detroit at near-even odds. Detroit ML at 1.98 clears the odds floor (1.65) comfortably. EV = (0.65 × 1.98) - 1 = 0.287. Stake reduced 20% for Cunningham OUT.

Current NBA official injury report does NOT list Wagner — he appears cleared and active. This is a material upgrade for Orlando. Meanwhile Toronto has THREE key players questionable: Brandon Ingram (Q-Inflammation), RJ Barrett (Q-Stiffness), Trayce Jackson-Davis (Q-Knee Tendinitis). Toronto (41-32, NetRtg +1.5) vs Orlando (39-34, NetRtg +0.8) — nearly identical records and ratings. With Toronto's depth at risk and Wagner likely active, Orlando at 2.04 offers genuine value. Line moved from 2.14 → 2.04 (-0.10) which is consistent with books partially adjusting for Wagner's availability, but hasn't fully priced in Toronto's multi-player questionable situation. Confidence build: Base 50 (even teams), Toronto home advantage offset (-5 for Orlando), Toronto 3 key players Q (+10), Wagner active upgrade (+10), Orlando's Isaac/Black OUT holes (-5) = 60. EV = (0.60 × 2.04) - 1 = +22.4%. Odds 2.04 in valid ML range (1.65-2.50). Injury source: nba_official (no cap restrictions). Time gate: 22:00 UTC tip, now 19:52 = 128 min > 20 min. scout_report_data.report:
**Miami Heat ML (2.08) — DRAFTED:** Philadelphia 76ers are the textbook 'hot streak with negative NetRtg' fade candidate explicitly identified in skills — 41-33 record but -0.2 NetRtg is deeply unsustainable. Miami is home, has a genuinely positive NetRtg (+2.2), and Powell/Rozier absences are matched by PHI's Broome absence. The fade rule triggers a +10 confidence boost pushing us to 62. EV 0.29 is a strong edge at these odds.

**San Antonio Spurs -19.0 (1.93) — DRAFTED:** NetRtg gap of 12.6 points between SAS (+8.1) and CHI (-4.5) is massive. Chicago is tank-watch territory (29-45, L10 3-7) with Simons, Collins, Ivey, Essengue all OUT — their roster is depleted. SAS has minimal meaningful injuries (all G-League callups). Tank teams historically don't cover spreads at double-digit numbers. EV 0.31 is strong.

**Oklahoma City Thunder -12.0 (1.89) — DRAFTED:** OKC is the best team in the league (NetRtg +11.0) hosting a Detroit team without Cade Cunningham (franchise player, OUT confirmed NBA PDF), Jalen Duren, Tobias Harris, Duncan Robinson, and Isaiah Stewart — five key rotation players absent. OKC has Jalen Williams out as well (confidence penalty applied), but SGA and Chet Holmgren remain. Detroit is essentially playing a reserve squad. EV 0.17 qualifies as marginal-to-strong edge.

Atlanta Hawks vs Boston Celtics — REJECTED: Jayson Tatum OUT confirmed; Boston ML confidence falls below 50 floor. Atlanta ML generates negative EV at 1.74. No valid pick.

Memphis Grizzlies vs Phoenix Suns — REJECTED: Both ML odds outside target range; spread confidence insufficient with Phoenix L10 4-6 and both teams carrying injuries.

Dallas Mavericks vs Minnesota Timberwolves — REJECTED: MIN ML 1.35 outside range; DAL confirmed tank with Kyrie/Lively OUT; Anthony Edwards Questionable complicates MIN thesis; no spread value on tanker.

Utah Jazz vs Cleveland Cavaliers — REJECTED: Jazz confirmed tank, skeleton roster; CLV ML 1.08 outside range; spread too wide given CLV's own depth depletion.

Los Angeles Lakers vs Washington Wizards — REJECTED: WAS confirmed clearest tank; LAL ML 1.08 outside range; LAL spread -16 undermined by Luka Doncic suspension — uncertain ability to cover large number without their primary star.

**Slate quality: Above average for spread betting.** The SAS vs CHI and OKC vs DET matchups offer genuine edges against depleted/tanking opponents. The PHI regression fade at Miami adds a quality ML edge. Three picks drafted, all with EV ≥ 0.15 — a strong session.

commit_report_data.report:
### CONFIRMED — Miami Heat ML
- Match: Miami Heat vs Philadelphia 76ers | Tip: 2026-03-30T23:00 UTC
- Pick: Miami Heat ML | Scout odds: 2.08 | Commit odds: 2.06 | Move: -0.02
- ML: H:2.06 / A:1.78 | ATS: -2.0 pts (1.91/1.91) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €55.50 | Pot: €114.33 | Conf: 62/100 | EV: 0.2772
- Decision: Minor 0.02 line move against (within 0.10 threshold, no investigation required). Both anchor players (Embiid, Butler) confirmed active on NBA official injury report — no new absences for either team since Scout. PHI NetRtg -0.2 with hot streak (L10 7-3, W% .554 > .550) remains the primary edge signal. Hot streak fade rule explicitly triggers: +10 confidence applied. Miami home advantage and positive NetRtg +2.2 supports the pick. EV 0.277 is strong. Confirmed at Medium tier (62 confidence), 15% bankroll stake.

---

### CANCELLED — Boston Celtics ML (Draft Pick 002)
- Reason: Franchise player Jayson Tatum confirmed OUT per NBA official injury report. Scout's own reasoning already identified rejection — confidence falls to 40 (65 base - 25 franchise player penalty), below minimum ML confidence floor of 50. Negative EV at -0.132. Additionally, Nikola Vucevic (new injury, not in Scout data), Ron Harper Jr., and Neemias Queta are all confirmed OUT at commit time, further deepening Boston's depletion. No path to confidence ≥ 50 on Boston ML.
- Odds at cancel: 2.17 (was 2.10 at Scout time — odds improved but irrelevant given rejection criteria)

---

### LATE PICK CONFIRMED — Atlanta Hawks -2.5
- Match: Atlanta Hawks vs Boston Celtics | Tip: 2026-03-30T23:30 UTC
- Pick: Atlanta Hawks -2.5 | Scout odds: N/A (late pick) | Commit odds: 1.88 | Move: N/A
- ML: H:1.71 / A:2.17 | ATS: -2.5 pts (1.88/1.94) | O/U: N/A
- Stake: €55.50 | Pot: €104.34 | Conf: 60/100 | EV: 0.128
- Decision: New late-breaking edge. Boston confirmed WITHOUT Tatum (OUT), Vucevic (OUT), Harper Jr. (OUT), Queta (OUT) — four players unavailable, including franchise player. Atlanta at full strength (only minor G-League and illness absences). Tatum's OUT is confirmed per NBA official (not Questionable/GTD), satisfying the injury-dependent pick rule — pick confirmed only because OUT is official. Atlanta home court advantage. Spread 1.88 within target range (1.80-2.30). All gates satisfied: confidence 60 ≥ 60 minimum, EV 0.128 > 0.05, time gate 63 min > 20 min, quantity gate (1st and only late pick). Atlanta ML at 1.71 showed negative EV (-0.008), but the spread at 1.88 generates sufficient EV given Atlanta covering just 2.5 points at home vs depleted road opponent.

---

### SLATE SUMMARY
Two picks committed totaling €111.00 (30.0% of €370.01 bankroll) — well within the 70% exposure cap. The core thesis centers on PHI's negative NetRtg regression risk in Miami (confirmed, both anchor players active) and Atlanta exploiting Boston's historic injury crisis at home on the spread. Draft pick 002 (BOS ML) was correctly self-cancelled by Scout and formally cancelled here; a superior alternative on the same game (ATL -2.5) was identified through the late scout process with stronger EV (0.128) and a confirmed injury catalyst.
